title: Tallyfy CLI settings
description: Configuration for the tallyfy CLI. Place at ~/.tallyfy/settings.json (user), .tallyfy/settings.json (project, committed), or .tallyfy/settings.local.json (project, personal). Scalar keys override by scope precedence; list keys (permissions, hooks, MCP servers) merge across scopes.

org.description: Default organization ID. Override per command with --org.

baseUrl.description: API base URL. Defaults to https://api.tallyfy.com.

permissions.description: Command permission rules. allow/ask/deny merge across scopes; deny always wins.

defaultMode.description: Decision for commands matched by no rule.

ask.description: Rules that prompt interactively (deny in non-interactive runs unless --yes).

deny.description: Rules to block. Wildcards allowed, e.g. "*(delete)".

allowManagedRulesOnly.description: Managed scope only: ignore all non-managed allow rules (lockdown).

mcp.description: MCP server enablement. enabledServers/disabledServers merge across scopes.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks keyed by event name (PreLaunch, PostComplete, PreDelete, and so on). Hooks merge across scopes and run only from trusted scopes.

matcher.description: Resource(verb) matcher; empty or "*" matches all.

command.description: Exec hooks: the command to run.

url.description: HTTP hooks: the endpoint to POST to (must pass the host allowlist).

env.description: Environment variables injected into hook and helper subprocesses.

apiKeyHelper.description: Path to a script that prints a token on stdout. Honored only from user, local, and managed scopes (or a trusted project).

forceOrg.description: Managed scope only: pin the organization for every command.

telemetry.description: Usage telemetry. Off by default. When enabled, emits only command name, duration, exit code, version, and OS.

requiredMinimumVersion.description: Managed scope only: refuse to run below this version.

requiredMaximumVersion.description: Managed scope only: refuse to run above this version.

allowedMcpServers.description: Managed scope only: restrict which MCP servers may be wired in.

deniedMcpServers.description: Managed scope only: MCP servers that may never be wired in.
